parsed.clarification_question: What is the primary focus of the review? Choose from: 1) Conceptual and logical validity, 2) Novelty and scholarship, 3) Structure and writing polish, 4) Comprehensive review including all aspects.

rationale.R1.1: Reviewing the paper section centrally involves conceptual reconstruction to reframe the thesis, gap statement, and contribution claim.
rationale.R1.2: Logical consistency checking of the argument chain is a central expected responsibility in a general section review.
rationale.R1.3: Aligning claims with reported evidence, such as the illustrations on datasets, is central to the review delegation.
rationale.R1.4: Assessing novelty against prior works like Krippendorff's α is centrally expected in reviewing the contribution.
rationale.R1.5: Evaluating and suggesting structural reorganization for better narrative arc is a central aspect of section review.
rationale.R1.6: Polishing writing at the sentence and paragraph level for grammar, flow, and conciseness is centrally requested.
rationale.R1.7: Checking citation accuracy, literature coverage, and attribution is central to scholarly review of the section.
rationale.RX.1: Ensuring proper uncertainty disclosure, such as in future work mentions, is a central cross-cutting responsibility in the review.
rationale.RX.2: Avoiding overclaims in the contribution and reliability statements is centrally important in reviewing the section.
rationale.RX.3: Adhering to the scope of ML annotation reliability is expected as a central element of the review.
rationale.RX.4: Considering downstream-harm avoidance in ML annotation contexts is a central responsibility for this review.
rationale.RX.5: Verifying provenance and traceability of mentioned datasets and methods is centrally expected in the review.
